steps.title: Identify the relationship between heights
steps.content:
The plant grows at a constant average rate over 14 days. The final height equals the initial height plus all the growth that occurred:

$$\text{Final height} = \text{Initial height} + \text{Total growth}$$

steps.title: Calculate the total growth
steps.content:
Growth occurs at 1.15 cm per day for 14 days:
$$\text{Total growth} = 1.15 \times 14 = 16.1 \text{ cm}$$

steps.title: Solve for initial height
steps.content:
Substitute into the relationship:
$$39.9 = \text{Initial height} + 16.1$$
$$\text{Initial height} = 39.9 - 16.1 = 23.8 \text{ cm}$$
